projects.title: Southside Taxiway – Phase 1 – Louisa County Airport / Freeman Field
projects.projectOwner: Industrial Development Authority of Louisa County
projects.description:
The Southside Taxiway – Phase 1 project at Louisa County Airport (Freeman Field) involves the construction of a new 400-foot parallel taxiway and a 240-foot connector taxiway. Funded through the FAA's Airport Improvement Program and supported by the Virginia Aviation Board, this initiative enhances the airport's airside infrastructure to support growing aircraft operations and ensure compliance with updated FAA design standards.
This project is a critical component of a multi-phase airport development plan aimed at improving safety, increasing operational capacity, and supporting regional economic development through improved aviation access.
scopeOfServices: Observation and Density Testing of Soil and Aggregate Base Materials during subgrade and base preparation.
Laboratory Testing of Soils: classification, Atterberg Limits, gradation, Standard Proctor, and CBR analysis to confirm design criteria.
Concrete Field Testing: slump, air content, unit weight, temperature, and compressive strength testing for airfield pavement and structural components.
Reinforcement Inspection: verification of steel bar placement, size, spacing, and coverage as per FAA and project specifications.
Asphalt Testing and Roller Pattern Verification to ensure compaction, mat thickness, and in-place density per FAA/VDOT requirements.
Daily Field Reports and Periodic Summary Documentation to track test results, identify nonconformance, and recommend corrective actions.
results: Ensured compliance with FAA, VDOT, and project-specific standards for all construction materials.
Improved safety and long-term durability of the new taxiway infrastructure.
Contributed to maintaining the project schedule and preventing costly rework by identifying deficiencies in real time.
Provided detailed documentation for project records, useful for FAA review and future maintenance planning.

projects.title: Walmart Distribution Center – Gordonsville, Louisa County, VA
projects.description:
The Walmart Distribution Center in Gordonsville is a state-of-the-art, 628,713-square-foot cold storage and distribution facility situated on a 68-acre site. The scope included preconstruction and construction services for a high-capacity perishable warehouse featuring temperature-controlled zones (-15°F freezer, 28°F meat, 34°F produce, and 55°F dairy), integrated with an Automated Storage and Retrieval System (ASRS).
Additional facilities included a truck maintenance garage, pallet processing center, fuel island, fire pump house, guardhouse, and a maintenance building. Testing and inspection services were critical due to the specialized temperature requirements and structural demands of the facility.
The project supports Walmart’s strategic logistics operations along key transportation corridors, enhancing inventory control and regional food delivery. Testing services ensured compliance with ASTM standards for structural integrity and thermal performance, vital for cold chain operations.
In 2024, plans were submitted to expand the facility with an additional warehouse, a 39,953-square-foot maintenance garage, new fuel bays with underground tanks, and expanded stormwater retention infrastructure.
scopeOfServices: Field and Laboratory Testing of Concrete in all cold zones (freezer, meat, dairy, produce) to ensure structural integrity and performance in sub-zero environments.
Soil Compaction Testing and evaluation of structural backfill supporting heavy equipment and warehouse foundations.
Asphalt Compaction and Thickness Testing for logistics truck lanes and staging areas.
Inspection of Reinforcing Steel and Concrete Placement to validate code compliance and construction sequencing.
Testing and Inspection of Ancillary Structures including fuel stations, fire pump facilities, maintenance buildings, and underground storage tanks.
results: Assurance of long-term durability and load-bearing performance.
Reliable temperature zone performance is critical to food safety and cold chain integrity.
Documentation and reporting that supported contractor milestones and regulatory approvals.
Support for phased expansion planning by establishing a verified baseline for soil conditions and structural compliance.

projects.title: Potomac Yard Station
projects.description: The Potomac Yard Metrorail Station is a major transit infrastructure project located approximately midway between the Ronald Reagan Washington National Airport and Braddock Road Stations along WMATA's Blue and Yellow Lines. The project is designed to improve local and regional transit accessibility to and from the Potomac Yard area adjacent to the U.S. Route 1 corridor.
scopeOfServices: Support of Excavation (SOE) design review and recommendations
Development and implementation of Geotechnical Instrumentation Monitoring Plan
Special Inspections (material verification, reinforcement, welding, anchors, formwork, deep foundations)
results: Comprehensive involvement in geotechnical design support and field quality control
Full conformance with WMATA standards and project specifications
Effective response to dynamic construction sequencing

projects.title: Edsall Road Fire Station
projects.description: The project consisted of expanding the existing Edsall Road Fire Station from approximately 8,300 SF to just over 14,000 SF on a 1.49-acre site. The new additions include four full-size apparatus bays with support space and a training tower, as well as a new kitchen and exercise room.
scopeOfServices: Soil Testing: Field density testing for subgrade preparation and structural fill placement
Concrete Testing: Slump, air content, temperature, and compressive strength testing
Masonry Inspection: Mortar, grout, and masonry unit testing

projects.title: Fairfax Police Tactical Operations Support Bureau (OSB)
projects.description: The project involves the demolition, site work, renovation, and construction of an addition to the Fairfax Police Tactical Operations Support Bureau (OSB). Originally constructed in the 1960s and last renovated in 1985, the existing 35,712 SF structure is being modernized and expanded to meet current and future operational demands.
scopeOfServices: Concrete field and lab testing to ensure mix design conformance
Materials compliance and reporting by project specifications

projects.title: Prestige Preschool Construction
projects.description: DGMTS provided third-party construction inspection and laboratory testing services for the Prestige Preschool Academy project in Sterling, VA. Our team conducted inspections and materials testing throughout key construction phases to ensure compliance with safety and quality standards.

projects.title: One Loudoun Landbay C Residential
projects.description: Dulles Geotechnical provided onsite material testing, foundation inspections, and laboratory analysis for the One Loudoun Landbay C Residential project by Miller & Smith. Our services ensured structural integrity, regulatory compliance, and construction quality across varying soil conditions.

projects.title: I-495 Express Lanes Northern Extension (I-495 NEXT)
projects.description: DGMTS provided geotechnical drilling, engineering support, construction inspection, and materials testing for the 2.5-mile I-495 Express Lanes Northern Extension (NEXT) project in Fairfax County, VA. Services included quality assurance, field investigations, and support for complex infrastructure components such as bridges, retaining walls, and toll systems.

projects.title: Transform I-66 – Outside the Beltway
projects.description: DGMTS supported the Transform 66 Outside the Beltway project by providing construction inspection and materials testing services across the 22.5-mile corridor. The project involved the construction of new express lanes, park-and-ride facilities, expanded transit access, and multimodal infrastructure.

projects.title: Route 7 Corridor Improvement Project
projects.description: DGMTS is providing geotechnical drilling, quality control inspection, and materials testing services for the Route 7 Corridor Improvement project. As a DBE partner to the Shirley-Dewberry Design-Build team, we are supporting major infrastructure enhancements including roadway widening, new bridges, sound barriers, and multi-use trails.

projects.title: Aston Martin Car Dealership
projects.description: DGMTS provided Quality Control Inspection, Materials Testing, and Geotechnical Engineering services for the construction of the Aston Martin Car Dealership in Loudoun County. Our team ensured that all construction components met the required standards for structural integrity, safety, and performance.

projects.title: Brown Mazda Car Dealership
projects.description: DGMTS provided Quality Control Inspection, Materials Testing, and Geotechnical Engineering services for the Brown Mazda Car Dealership project in Fairfax. Our role ensured that construction adhered to design specifications, safety standards, and regulatory compliance from the ground up.

projects.title: City Hall Redevelopment for the City of College Park and the University of Maryland
projects.projectOwner: University of Maryland and City of College Park
projects.description: This 90,000-square-foot mixed-use civic facility was developed as a joint effort between the City of College Park and the University of Maryland. The building houses city government offices, university departments, retail storefronts, and a large public plaza. Designed with Class-A standards and sustainability in mind.
scopeOfServices: Building Envelope Assessments (curtainwall, waterproofing, EIFS, roofing)
Concrete and Rebar Inspection and Testing
Utility and Sitework Testing (trench backfill, compaction, paving)

projects.title: Bladensburg Library
projects.description: The Bladensburg Library is undergoing major redevelopment involving the complete demolition of the existing facility and construction of a new, significantly larger and more modern public library. The new facility, designed to be nearly three times the size of the previous structure, aims to better serve the Prince George's County community.
scopeOfServices: Floor Flatness (FF) and Floor Levelness (FL) testing services

projects.title: Safe Routes to School (SRTS) – Countryside Elementary School
projects.client: F.H. Paschen, S.N. Nielsen & Associates LLC
projects.location: Triple seven Road, Loudoun County, VA
projects.description: The Safe Routes to School (SRTS) project at Countryside Elementary School in Loudoun County, Virginia, was designed to improve pedestrian safety and accessibility for students. The project constructed approximately 900 feet of new sidewalks, trails, and crosswalks along the east side of Triple seven Road.
scopeOfServices: Soil Compaction Testing and Concrete Inspection
Field Concrete Testing and Compressive Strength Lab Analysis
Special Inspections: Welding, Anchor Bolts, Rebar, Formwork, Soil, and Deep Foundations
Material Verification for Multiple Facilities on Site

projects.title: Maury Elementary School Modernization
projects.description: The Maury Elementary School Modernization Project, a $52 million D.C. Public Schools initiative, involved a comprehensive renovation of the historic 1886 classroom building and the construction of a new three-story addition. The modernization expanded capacity for preschool through fifth grade students while creating a 21st-century learning environment.
scopeOfServices: Geotechnical engineering services including subsurface investigation and laboratory testing
Soil compaction testing and concrete inspection during construction
Quality control and inspection services to ensure compliance
Support for integration of historic structure with new construction

projects.title: River Renew Tunnel System Project
projects.description: DGMTS is providing third-party quality control inspection and testing services for the RiverRenew Tunnel System Project, a major infrastructure initiative by Alexandria Renew Enterprises to eliminate combined sewer overflows (CSOs) and improve water quality in local waterways. The project includes deep shafts, tunnels, diversion sewers, and upgrades to the AlexRenew Water Resource Recovery Facility.
scopeOfServices: Surface Facility and Underground Structure Monitoring
Reporting, Deficiency Tracking, and Corrective Action Recommendations

projects.title: Leesburg Executive Airport Apron Paving
projects.description: This project involved comprehensive site work and paving for the south terminal apron at Leesburg Executive Airport, including tie-ins to all adjoining taxiways and two corporate hangars. The main apron measures approximately 880' x 440', totaling roughly 43,000 square yards.
scopeOfServices: Placement of new stone base and federal P-401 asphalt surface course
Demolition and re-alignment of existing steel tie-downs
Installation of conduit and junction boxes for future electrical needs
Site preparation for future land leases

projects.title: Morven Park Road Sidewalk & Drainage Improvements
projects.description: The Morven Park Road Sidewalk and Drainage Improvements project provides continuous pedestrian access along Morven Park Road between West Market Street and Old Waterford Road. Improvements include installation of missing sidewalk links, new curb and gutter on both sides, and storm drain extensions.
scopeOfServices: Construction management and field quality control
Oversight of curb, gutter, sidewalk, and storm drain installation
Coordination with utility providers (Dominion, Verizon, Comcast, Washington Gas)
Traffic control oversight and maintenance of resident driveway access

projects.title: Safford Brown Hyundai Dealership
projects.description: This project involves the ground-up construction of a 32,000-square-foot Hyundai dealership and service facility on a 5.6-acre site in Leesburg, VA. The facility will provide expanded vehicle sales and service operations in a state-of-the-art environment.
scopeOfServices: Construction inspection and materials testing services
Oversight of site grading, foundations, and utility installations
Monitoring of structural concrete, asphalt, and paving operations
Field inspections and reporting to ensure compliance with design specifications
Coordination with the general contractor and design team throughout construction

projects.title: ANC Yellow Line - Instrumentation, Alington Virginia
projects.description: In September 2019, the Army Corps of Engineers released the Draft Finding of No Significant Impact for the Southern Expansion and Associated Roadway Realignment Final Environmental Assessment. The Final Environmental Assessment evaluated potential impacts resulting from the proposed increase of the cemetery's contiguous acreage and roadway realignment by utilizing the adjacent, former Navy Annex site located just south of the existing cemetery in Arlington, Virginia. It also described the potential historical effects of integrating the Air Force Memorial with the cemetery as part of the expansion.

projects.title: Summit School Road - Geotechnical Instrumentation and Monitoring
servicesProvided: Settlement Monitoring of the Concrete Retaining Walls
projects.description: Following a groundbreaking ceremony, Lake Ridge's Summit School Road project is now underway and marks the county's infrastructure development. This 1.2-mile extension stretches from Kinnicutt Drive to Telegraph Road and will be transformed into a four-lane divided roadway. The project will widen Telegraph Road from Caton Hill Road to Prince William Parkway, increasing it from two to four lanes. Enhancements include a raised grass median, a five-foot sidewalk, and a ten-foot shared-use path to accommodate additional pedestrian traffic.
scopeOfServices: Settlement Monitoring of the Concrete Retaining Walls

projects.title: ANC DAR-B/C Vibration Monitoring - Geotechnical Instrumentation and Monitoring
projects.description: The Federal Highway Administration (FHWA) Eastern Federal Lands Highway Division is designing the roadways that would be relocated as a result of Cemetery expansion. This project will realign Columbia Pike from east of South Oak Street to Washington Boulevard, modify the South Joyce Street intersection and the Columbia Pike/Washington Boulevard (Route 27) interchange, and removes a segment of Southgate Road and builds a new South Nash Street.\nThe project also includes a new multi-use trail adjacent to the cemetery, pedestrian and bicycle facilities, and a new tunnel between the cemetery and its new maintenance compound.

projects.title: VPRA Long Bridge North: Phase 1 Instrumentation and Monitoring
projects.description: The Long Bridge Project includes the construction of a new two-track rail bridge over the Potomac River ( " Potomac River Undergrade Bridge " ). This new bridge will be constructed adjacent to the existing CSXT owned George Washington Memorial Parkway (GWMP) Bridge and Long Bridge. The overall proposed construction involves building a new bridge alongside the Long Bridge. Dulles Geotechnical and Material Testing Services, Inc. (DGMTS) is retained by Skanska to develop an Instrumentation and Monitoring (I&M) program in support of the Early Work Program #3 (EWP#3) for Long Bridge North Package. Instrumentation included Track monitoring and the structures monitoring by using AMTS, Seismographs and Tiltmeters.

projects.title: Sheridan, Wilson, McClellan Road, Repairs Arlington National Cemetery, Arlington, Virginia – Geotechnical Engineering Services
servicesProvided: Pre and Post Construction Condition Survey
projects.description: This project at the historic Arlington National Cemetery involved replacement / rehabilitation of several roadways including Sheridan, Wilson, McClellan, and Weeks / Grant Loop drives, along with drainage improvements. The scope also covered rehabilitating Crooks Walk, its bridge, Red Spring areas, and improving the Kennedy Memorial Tram Stop. Key elements included addressing site conditions, surveying, evaluating environmental needs, enhancing pedestrian access and drainage systems, protecting historic structures / trees, and providing phased construction plans. Design work focused on safety, curb / underdrain installations, utility adjustments, tree preservation, erosion control plans, and pedestrian area enhancements like step/handrail replacements and sidewalk improvements.
scopeOfServices: Pre and Post Construction Condition Survey

projects.title: Arlington National Cemetery Southern Expansion, Arlington, Virginia – Geotechnical Instrumentation (DGMTS project No. 21261)
projects.location: South Nash Street and Columbia Pike
servicesProvided: Pre and Post Construction Condition Survey
projects.description: The Southern Expansion construction project began with a groundbreaking ceremony for Phase I on September 14, 2021. The 50-acre Southern Expansion area is bound by Washington Boulevard (Route 27), I-395, the Virginia Department of Transportation (VDOT) Maintenance Complex, the Foxcroft Heights neighborhood and Arlington National Cemetery. Construction of Phase II and Phase III of the Southern Expansion project is anticipated to occur over the next five years (spring 2023 through winter 2027) and will add more than 80,000 new interment opportunities. Additionally, the project will significantly enhance the experience of visitors to ANC, while preserving the iconic look and feel of this national shrine. Benefits to visitors include a new entrance on Columbia Pike; employee parking and controlled public parking for cemetery and Air Force Memorial visitors; and seamless access to the Air Force Memorial. Finally, Southern Expansion will facilitate ANC operations by replacing an office building and a warehouse, and adding green-roof maintenance bays for cemetery vehicles
scopeOfServices: Pre and Post Construction Condition Survey

projects.title: Washington Union Station, Amtrak Track 22 Rehabilitation, Washington DC (DGMTS 20078)
projects.description: The rehabilitation of Track 22 at Washington Union Station is a significant upgrade aimed at enhancing the station's capacity and service reliability. The project involved the installation of new tracks, rebuilding the platform, and rehabilitating the historic columns at the 118-year-old station. The new platform is fully accessible and features a new passageway with an elevator, escalator, and stairs, ensuring convenient access to and from trains. Track 22 primarily serves Virginia Railway Express (VRE) trains, as well as Amtrak Long Distance and Amtrak Virginia services, connecting the Northeast Corridor with destinations across the Southeast. This investment is crucial for easing rail congestion and improving service reliability at Amtrak's second busiest station in Washington, D.C.

projects.title: Potomac Yards Metrorail Station
projects.description: The project is the Potomac Yard Metrorail Station located in Alexandria, Virginia. The station will be built on Metrorail's Yellow and Blue Lines between the existing Braddock Road and Ronald Reagan Washington National Airport stations. The station is at-grade with side platforms between the George Washington Parkways and the CSX tracks, north of Potomac Greens and east of the existing Potomac Yard Retail Center and CSX right-of-way. The project includes a new switchgear building, a pedestrian bridge over CSX tracks to the pavilion, and a pavilion.

metadata.rfpTitle: Geotechnical Engineering, Construction Inspection and Testing Services
metadata.companyName: Dulles Geotechnical and Material Testing Services, Inc. (DGMTS)
